description_long: This object is a classic revolver-style firearm with a long, metallic barrel and a rounded, six-chambered cylinder. The barrel and frame are made of aged, slightly tarnished metal, showing signs of wear and engraving details along the sides. The grip is crafted from polished dark wood, ergonomically shaped for a comfortable hold, and contrasts with the metallic components. The hammer is exposed and cocked back, and the trigger is housed within a rounded metal guard. The muzzle is circular and the overall design is reminiscent of 19th-century Colt revolvers, often seen in historical or Western urban settings. The object is compact, with intricate mechanical parts visible, including the cylinder, hammer, and trigger assembly.
description: A classic metallic revolver with a long barrel, engraved details, and a polished wooden grip. The design features an exposed hammer and a rounded trigger guard.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, engraved metalwork, and the polished wooden grip.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the rounded wooden grip and the alignment of the barrel and cylinder.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the metallic frame, cylinder, and the wooden grip from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the circular muzzle, barrel opening, and the front sight.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long and round with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metal guard with smooth edges and no finger grooves
